the_pit.name: The Pit
the_pit.vibe: A drained coolant cistern three levels under the Grid. Scaffold seating bolted to the tank walls, one ring of scavenged stadium light, and a brass ship's bell that decides everything. The crowd never forgets a name.
the_pit.closed_line: The hatch is chained and the tank below is dark. A hand-lettered sign: FIRST BELL AT 16:00.
the_pit.enter_line: You climb down the service ladder into heat, noise, and the smell of ozone and old blood.

the_hold.name: The Hold
the_hold.vibe: A gutted cargo hold refitted as a gym: crane-counterweight machines, chalk dust hanging in the lamplight, and a brine tank bolted to the aft wall with the head coach living in it.
the_hold.closed_line: The Hold's shutter is down. Chalk-scrawled hours: 06:00–23:00.
the_hold.enter_line: The smell of chalk, salt, and honest effort. Somewhere aft, water sloshes approvingly.

training.blurb: House rates: Agility and Courage train under coaching here — one hour, −8 energy, instead of two and −15.

gantry_9.name: Gantry 9
gantry_9.vibe: The rooftop-line terminus, nine floors above the Grid: a crane gantry glassed in against the wind, kettles riding induction coils, and the whole city spread out below like a live circuit diagram. It never closes; the tea just gets stronger after midnight.
gantry_9.enter_line: Wind, then the door, then kettle-steam and quiet. Nine floors down, the Grid hums like it's thinking.

trait_lines.avian: You skip the stairs and drop onto the landing rail. The regulars don't look up — Rooftop Liners always come in on the wind.

night_market.name: The Night Market
night_market.vibe: At dusk the empty lot blooms: stalls under strings of salvaged biolights, steam and static and six languages of haggling. Everything is fresh, nothing has a receipt.
night_market.closed_line: By day it's chalk outlines of stalls and the smell of last night's smoke. Vendors drift back at 18:00.
night_market.enter_line: Biolight and broth-steam. Somewhere to your left, someone is winning an argument about fish.
